mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value 101.61 as of 2015-09 is above long-term trend
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO trailing 3-month change vs forward 60-day returns is below +0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6%, it signals labor market slack sufficient to depress consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 9.7 in 2015-09, well above 6% threshold
failure_condition: If after trigger activation, forward 20-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates strong consumer and construction demand, amplifying each other to produce robust economic expansion and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST have positive 3-month growth rates
failure_condition: If the joint signal leads to positive returns less than 60% of the time when both proxies are rising, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (federal funds rate above 4%), falling housing starts signal that interest rate sensitive sectors are weakening, foreshadowing broader economic contraction and negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS>4% and HOUST declining month-over-month, the forward 5-day returns are positive more than 40% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payroll growth exceeds 200,000, it indicates robust labor demand supporting consumer income and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns are positive in less than 60% of triggered months, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth provides a lagging but fundamental confirmation of economic health; above-trend GDP growth supports corporate earnings and leads to positive returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rolling 2-year correlation between GDPC1 annualized growth and forward 60-day returns is negative or below 0.1, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production and retail sales are accelerating simultaneously, it indicates synchronized strength in supply and demand, creating a powerful positive impulse for equity returns over the short-term (5-day horizon).
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS have positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: If when both proxies are rising, the forward 5-day return is negative more than 40% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in consumer price index above 2% annual inflation signals overheating and potential monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods with CPI YoY > 2%, forward 20-day returns are positive more than 40% of the time, reject.
